revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八王子神社 (さいたま市)]]
八王子神社 (さいたま市) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Hachioji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Saitama Hachioji Shrine Haiden 201901 1.jpg |A11ress=[[Saitama Prefecture|Saitama]] [[Saitama City|Saitama City]] {{ill|Chūō-ku, Saitama|en|Chūō-ku, Saitama|qid=Q976945|lt=Chūō-ku|1=Chūō-ku, Saitama}} Hachioji 1-7-11 |Latitude=35 |Latitude minutes=53 |Latitude seconds=09.4 |Longitude=139 |Longitude minutes=36 |Longitude seconds=47 |deity={{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正勝吾勝勝速日天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q462555}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|天之穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津彦根命|1=Ikutsuhikone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11147583}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi|ja|熊野橡樟日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano-Kusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Historical Rank=Village Shrine |Established=Unknown |prefecture={{ill|Chuo-ku|qid=Q976945|1=Chūō-ku, Saitama}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.88594444444445|N|139.61305555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Hachioji Shrine''' (八王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Saitama|ja|さいたま市中央区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūō-ku, Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中央区 (さいたま市)|1=Chūō-ku, Saitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q976945}}, {{ill|Saitama (city)|en|Saitama (city)|qid=Q134635|lt=Saitama City|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. Within the same grounds lies the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Saitama)|ja|浅間神社 (さいたま市中央区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine (Saitama)|1=Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q134986809}} of Yono; however, the two are independent entities, with separate legal statuses as {{ill|Religious corporation|lt=Religious corporation|lt=Religious corporations|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/religious corporations|qq=}}<ref name="埼玉県">[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/2035/r3pdfsyukyouhoujinichiran.pdf 埼玉県知事所轄宗教法人一覧], 埼玉県総務部学事課総務・宗教法人担当, 5p.</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown. However, in the {{ill|Yūzu Nenbutsu Engi Emaki|ja|融通念仏縁起|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūzu Nenbutsu Engi Emaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which records the deeds of {{ill|Ryōnin|en|Ryōnin|qid=Q11614413|lt=Ryonin|1=Ryōnin}}, founder of the {{ill|Yuzu Nembutsu|en|Yuzu Nembutsu|qid=Q578719|lt=Yūzu Nenbutsu sect|1=Yuzu Nembutsu}}, it is noted that during the {{ill|Shōka (era)|en|Shōka (era)|qid=Q1327489|lt=Shōka era|1=Shōka (era)}} (1257–1259), a local {{ill|headman|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/headman|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q110095935}} in Yono recited nenbutsu, which purportedly protected his family from an epidemic. Ryonin, originally a monk of {{ill|Mount Hiei|en|Mount Hiei|qid=Q748040|lt=Mount Hiei|1=Mount Hiei}}'s {{ill|Enryaku-ji|en|Enryaku-ji|qid=Q917195|lt=Enryaku-ji|1=Enryaku-ji}}, was associated with the {{ill|Hachioji Gongen|ja|八王子権現|1=Hachiōji Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Gongen|qq=}}, which had strong ties to Enryaku-ji. It is believed that itinerant monks spreading Yūzu Nenbutsu in the medieval period enshrined the Hachioji Gongen here<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団, 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁, 1998, 444–445p.</ref>.
The shrine was once affiliated with a temple, {{ill|Tōkaku-in|ja|東覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaku-in|1=Togakuin temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97172540}}, which acted as its {{ill|Bettōji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Bettō-ji|qid=Q11396755}}. Tōkaku-in was part of the {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|qid=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}} {{ill|Honzan-ha|ja|本山派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ha|1=Honzan-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11520192}}, but the temple was abolished during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} of the Meiji era. The temple's monks subsequently {{ill|secularized|ja|還俗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/secularized|1=laicization in Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2583238}} and became priests of the shrine<ref name="神社"></ref>.
In {{ill|1873|en|1873|qid=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated a "Village Shrine" under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}<ref name="神社"></ref>.
== Transportation == * Approximately 22 minutes on foot from either {{ill|Kita-Yono Station|en|Kita-Yono Station|qid=Q844444|lt=Kita-Yono Station|1=Kita-Yono Station}} or {{ill|Yonohommachi Station|en|Yonohommachi Station|qid=Q844390|lt=Yono-Hommachi Station|1=Yonohommachi Station}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁, 1998
